Visit Palazzo Molteni, via Manzoni 9 
By appointment only

body:
Letters to Milan, an installation by @studioklass 
8-13 April 2025
Museo Poldi Pezzoli, Via Manzoni 12, Milan

Milan has witnessed the birth and growth of Molteni&C in constant dialogue with leading figures in architecture and design: Gio Ponti, Bruno Morassutti and Angelo Mangiarotti, Aldo Rossi, and Herzog & de Meuron. Letters to Milan celebrates this legacy by transforming the space into a metaphor for the city. 

Open to the public: Museo Poldi Pezzoli, Via Manzoni 12, Milan
Visiting hours:
Wednesday: 10am – 7.30pm
Thursday – Friday: 10am – 6.30pm
Saturday: 10am – 9pm
Sunday: 10am – 7pm

Quote curated by Doppiozero @00doppiozero 

#MolteniGroup #MDW25

body:
2025 Collection Preview: Emile sofa system by Christophe Delcourt

An expression of modular elegance, Emile by Christophe Delcourt from the 2025 Collection is a dynamic sofa system designed to adapt to the evolving needs of contemporary living. 

Characterized by a sculptural silhouette, Emile features a refined backrest with fluid, shifting heights, creating a harmonious play of proportions within the space. Its distinctive graphic marks trace its form, ensuring a striking presence from every perspective. 

The new Collection will be unveiled during Milan Design Week 2025, 8-13 April. 
Book your visit at the link in bio.

@christophedelcourt 

#MolteniGroup #NewCollection #design #MDW25

Characterized by a sculptural silhouette, the Emile sofa by Christophe Delcourt from the 2025 Collection features a refined backrest with fluid, shifting heights, creating a harmonious play of proportions within the space. 

It finds a perfect complement in the Odile small tables, designed to be integrated within the seating system or used as standalone elements. Their contrasting materials and geometric forms enhance the versatility of the composition, enriching the overall aesthetic with functional refinement. 

The new Collection will be unveiled during Milan Design Week 2025, 8-13 April. 
Book your visit at the link in bio.

@christophedelcourt

body:
Letters to Milan, an installation by @studioklass 
8-13 April 2025
Museo Poldi Pezzoli, Via Manzoni 12, Milan

Letters to Milan tells the story of the deep connection between Milan and Molteni&C, a dialogue built over time through architecture, design, and the way of living in the home. It’s a story of space and language, which also lends voice to the words of three authors, Umberto Fiori, Giorgio Fontana and Federica Fracassi, through the writers they have chosen to talk about Milan.

Open to the public: Museo Poldi Pezzoli, Via Manzoni 12, Milan
Visiting hours: 
Wednesday: 10am – 7.30pm
Thursday – Friday: 10am – 6.30pm
Saturday: 10am – 9pm
Sunday: 10am – 7pm

Quote curated by Doppiozero @00doppiozero 

#MolteniGroup #MDW25 #MilanoDesignWeek

body:
Composing comfort with Turner

An invitation to transform living spaces into havens of comfort, the Turner sofa by Hannes Wettstein is a refined piece that harmonizes with any contemporary aesthetic. Designed to easily shapeshift for the enhancement of relaxation and style, Turner combines a flexible array of settees, chaises longues, poufs, and corner pieces to create a sofa design that can evolve over time, defining the space as it caters thoughtfully to personal preference. A simple, built-in mechanism adjusts the sofa’s seat depths, enhancing the comfortable possibilities.

body:
Monk chair, the re-edition

First introduced into the Molteni&C Collection in 1973, the Monk chair by Afra and Tobia Scarpa is defined by its elegant subtlety. Two refined materials, wood and leather, are crafted into a clean silhouette held together by visible screws. The pared-down nature of the design draws inspiration from monastic simplicity, for which it has been named.

The Monk chair is now available as an exclusive re-edition in the 2025 Collection.

body:
Letters to Milan, an installation by @studioklass 
8-13 April 2025
Museo Poldi Pezzoli, Via Manzoni 12, Milan

The installation celebrates Milan and its ever-changing ideals of modernity interpreted by the architects who have shaped the city – Gio Ponti, Angelo Mangiarotti and Bruno Morassutti, Aldo Rossi, Herzog & de Meuron. In a narrative between spaces and languages, the installation gives voice to authors Umberto Fiori, Federica Fracassi, and Giorgio Fontana, highlighting their writings about Milan. An evocation, a song, a prayer, a tribute, a supplication, an incantation, a dedication.

Open to the public: Museo Poldi Pezzoli, Via Manzoni 12, Milan
Visiting hours: 
Wednesday: 10am – 7.30pm
Thursday – Friday: 10am – 6.30pm
Saturday: 10am – 9pm
Sunday: 10am – 7pm

Quote curated by Doppiozero @00doppiozero 

#MolteniGroup #MDW25

body:
Palazzo Molteni Tokyo

After the successful grand opening of Palazzo Molteni in Milan, we are honored to inaugurate Palazzo Molteni Tokyo, the new Flagship Store in Minami Aoyama, designed by Vincent Van Duysen.

The architectural concept for Palazzo Molteni Tokyo, was to create a façade that reflects the layout of the interior and domestic spaces, while still being perceived as a single sculptural entity.

“When I started thinking about this project, I wanted to create a very sculptural volume, monolithic…we could also describe it as tectonic, where there is an interplay between masses and voids. We aimed to express something very geometric in terms of patterns and playfulness yet using a very restrained and quiet architectural language.”
– Vincent Van Duysen

@vincentvanduysen

body:
Palazzo Molteni Tokyo

Now celebrating its grand opening, Palazzo Molteni Tokyo is conceived as a sculpture — an architectural expression of the Molteni&C home and lifestyle, in the heart of the Japanese capital. The building is the first architectural project in Japan by Belgian architect Vincent Van Duysen, Molteni&C’s Creative Director since 2016.

The façade is defined by a refined, elegant composition of solids and voids, as if sculpted from a monolithic block.

A sequence of stacked, offset blocks — interrupted by darkened voids and modernist-inspired metallic elements — creates a sense of rhythm and depth. Visually, the façade appears dynamic yet never repetitive, with each side offering a unique perspective.

#MolteniGroup #Design

Monk chair, the re-edition

Originally designed in 1973, Monk was part of Afra and Tobia Scarpa’s first collaboration with Molteni&C and an early expression of a partnership that would leave a lasting impact on modern design. It emerged during a time when Italian radical design was pushing bold, experimental forms, yet Monk remained rooted in tradition, prioritizing material integrity and a rational design approach.

The Monk chair is now available as an exclusive re-edition in the 2025 Collection.

Composing comfort with Turner

Thoughtfully designed for adjustable comfort, the Turner sofa by Hannes Wettstein can be composed to fit the dimensions of any living space. Offering an array of chaise lounges, corner pieces, poufs, and settees in a range of sizes, Turner’s responsive design combines graceful lines with a rich, customized feel. Enhanced with a mechanism to adjust the depth of its seating, this timeless sofa creates an inviting ambiance with a flair of sophistication.

The Emile sofa, inspired by sculpture.

Drawing inspiration from mid-century spatialist art, the Emile sofa by Christophe Delcourt from the 2025 Collection enriches the aesthetic of the living space with its sculptural form.

Adorned by distinctive graphic lines, the modular sofa system is a visual masterpiece when viewed from any angle, making it a compelling addition to the center of the living space. 

The sofa’s fluid backrest heights and alternating seat cushion depths create a silhouette embodying a sense of movement.

body:
Palazzo Molteni Tokyo

Now celebrating its grand opening, Palazzo Molteni Tokyo is conceived as a sculpture — an architectural expression of the Molteni&C home and lifestyle, in the heart of the Japanese capital. The building is the first architectural project in Japan by Belgian architect Vincent Van Duysen, Molteni&C’s Creative Director since 2016.

The façade is defined by a refined, elegant composition of solids and voids, as if sculpted from a monolithic block.

A sequence of stacked, offset blocks — interrupted by darkened voids and modernist-inspired metallic elements — creates a sense of rhythm and depth. Visually, the façade appears dynamic yet never repetitive, with each side offering a unique perspective.

#MolteniGroup #Design
